nodes.name: 山麻雀
nodes.summary: 山麻雀（Russet sparrow，学名 Passer cinnamomeus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联12个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原。

nodes.name: 布拉马普特拉河
nodes.summary: 布拉马普特拉河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括红胸山鹧鸪、斑头绿拟啄木鸟、恒河沙百灵等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 不丹
nodes.summary: 不丹是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联156种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、红胸山鹧鸪、红胸角雉等156种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 尼泊尔
nodes.summary: 尼泊尔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联193种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、环颈山鹧鸪、红胸角雉等193种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 千島群島
nodes.summary: 千島群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括牛头伯劳、杂色山雀、烟腹毛脚燕等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 北阿坎德邦
nodes.summary: 北阿坎德邦是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括针尾绿鸠、尼泊尔鹪鹛、灰背燕尾等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 托海
nodes.summary: 托海是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联22种鸟类，包括红头林鹧鸪、Cocos cuckoo、Malaysian eared nightjar等22种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 喜马拉雅山脉
nodes.summary: 喜马拉雅山脉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联124种鸟类，包括斑头雁、琵嘴鸭、雪鹑等124种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 喜马偕尔邦
nodes.summary: 喜马偕尔邦是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括細嘴兀鷲、林鵰、尼泊尔鹪鹛等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 平均海拔
nodes.summary: 平均海拔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Andean guan、鳳冠孔雀雉等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 东亚
nodes.summary: 东亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联17种鸟类，包括花脸鸭、日本鵪鶉、大鸨等17种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 北海道
nodes.summary: 北海道是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联13种鸟类，包括丹顶鹤、赤颈鸊鷉、普通秧鸡等13种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 库页岛
nodes.summary: 库页岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括小青脚鹬、北领角鸮、雪鸮等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The subspecies rutilans and intensior breed in parts of eastern Asia, where they are usually found in light woodland, and the subspecies cinnamomeus breeds in the Himalayas, where it is usually associated with terrace cultivation

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The subspecies rutilans and intensior breed in parts of eastern Asia, where they are usually found in light woodland, and the subspecies cinnamomeus breeds in the Himalayas, where it is usually associated with terrace cultivation

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is not known whether its distribution is continuous between these two areas, since the political sensitivity of the Brahmaputra River's valley near the China-India border makes the area less accessible to ornithologists. In the Himalayas the russet sparrow breeds from the far northeast of India through southeastern Tibet, Bhutan, Sikkim, Nepal, Uttarakhand, and Himachal Pradesh to Kashmir and Nuristan in Afghanistan. Here, the russet sparrow makes short movements to lower altitudes between November and April in many areas. In eastern Asia, the russet sparrow is found in Sakhalin, the Kuril Islands, a small part of mainland Russia, Japan, southern Korea, and part of northern China, where it is mostly migratory

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: This preference for high altitudes is influenced by latitude: in the farthest south of its range it never breeds below above mean sea level, but in the northernmost part of its range it often breeds by the sea. In eastern Asia, the russet sparrow prefers light woodland, but it is sometimes found in towns and agricultural areas. In Sakhalin, it breeds mostly in riparian forest. In Hokkaidō, the russet sparrow finds a greater food supply for its young in more remote forests, and has greater reproductive success. In the Himalayas, it is strongly associated with terrace cultivation, and it probably only spread to the Himalayas when these agricultural practices arrived 3000 to 4000 years ago. In towns where it occurs alongside the house or tree sparrow, it is found in gardens and less built up areas. In those hill stations in India where both the house and the russet sparrow occur, the house sparrow breeds around more-built up areas and bazaars, whereas the russet sparrow is "rather more up market at the larger houses with gardens and open spaces". In towns where it is the only sparrow, it is not dependent on trees, breeding around houses and eating scraps in streets. In the winter, migratory birds occur in open cultivated land and riverine grasslands, but are never far from shrubs or trees
